name: Plazaola Greenway and Leitzaran Valley Loop from Tolosa
name_original: Vía Verde del Plazaola – circular desde Tolosa por Andoain
description: Cycle the difficult 31.6-mile Vía Verde del Plazaola circular route from Tolosa, featuring tunnels, viaducts, and the Leitzaran River Valley
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Tolosa traverse the green valleys and mountains of the Basque Country in Spain. The region is characterized by verdant landscapes, including the Leitzaran Protected Biotope and the Aralar Natural Park, offering diverse terrain for cyclists. Rivers like the Oria and Leitzaran carve through the area, providing scenic backdrops for many routes. This varied topography supports a range of cycling experiences, from gentle greenways to challenging mountain ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Tolosa The most popular touring cycling route is Plazaola Greenway and Leitzaran Valley Loop from Tolosa, a 31.6 miles (50.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows an old railway line through green valleys, offering views towards the Pyrenees. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Rest and Recreation Area – Old Bridge loop from Tolosa, a moderate 24.0 miles (38.7 km) path. This route provides a scenic journey, often completed in about 2 hours 19 minutes. Local touring cyclists also love the Lizarrusti Pass – Old Bridge loop from Tolosa, a 45.7 miles (73.6 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 37 minutes. Touring cycling around Tolosa is defined by green valleys, picturesque mountains, and natural parks like Aralar. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to demanding climbs. The routes in Tolosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tolosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Tolosa?
A: Tolosa offers a diverse selection of over 20 touring cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy rides to challenging mountain ascents through the region's green valleys and mountains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Tolosa?
A: The terrain around Tolosa is varied, featuring verdant valleys, picturesque mountains, and natural parks like Aralar. You'll find everything from mostly paved greenways, such as sections of the Plazaola Greenway, to demanding climbs with significant elevation gains, some of which may include unpaved sections, especially in natural spaces like Aralar Natural Park.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Tolosa?
A: Yes, Tolosa offers options for easier rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40641304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de Basasiche – Old Bridge loop from Tolosa-Centro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.6 km path. The Plazaola Greenway, a significant part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085610707/plazaola-greenway-and-leitzaran-valley-loop-from-tolosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plazaola Greenway and Leitzaran Valley Loop from Tolosa&lt;/a&gt;, is also largely paved and suitable for all skill levels, making it ideal for a family outing.

Q: Which routes offer challenging climbs for experienced touring cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38257190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lizarrusti Pass – Old Bridge loop from Tolosa&lt;/a&gt; provide significant elevation gains and demanding slopes. The Alto de Iturburu is another popular cycling climb known for its uneven gradients and green countryside views, often featured in professional races. Routes in Aralar Natural Park also offer tougher, more demanding experiences.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Tolosa's routes are rich in natural beauty and landmarks. You can cycle through the scenic Leitzaran Protected Biotope, enjoy views of the Pyrenees along the Plazaola Greenway, and see the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1750262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Witches' Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The region is characterized by its 'marvellous greenery,' with routes often bounded by rivers like the Oria and Leitzaran. For panoramic views, consider routes that ascend towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uzturre Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Tolosa?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Tolosa are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085610707/plazaola-greenway-and-leitzaran-valley-loop-from-tolosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plazaola Greenway and Leitzaran Valley Loop from Tolosa&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38251588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest and Recreation Area – Old Bridge loop from Tolosa&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38257190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lizarrusti Pass – Old Bridge loop from Tolosa&lt;/a&gt;, offering convenient starting and ending points.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Tolosa?
A: The touring cycling routes in Tolosa are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 3.8 stars from numerous reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from gentle greenways to challenging mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3083575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lizarrusti Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and the consistently scenic backdrop of green valleys and mountains.

Q: Can I explore historical or cultural sites while cycling in Tolosa?
A: Absolutely. Tolosa itself boasts a rich historical and cultural heritage. Cyclists can explore its old town, the Plaza Nueva, and the medieval bridge. The town also features numerous parks and gardens, offering a cultural dimension to your touring trip. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1750262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Witches' Bridge&lt;/a&gt; along the Plazaola Greenway is another notable historical point of interest.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Tolosa?
A: Tolosa's 'marvellous greenery' is a direct result of its local climate, suggesting pleasant conditions for cycling through much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer mild temperatures and beautiful landscapes, ideal for exploring the green valleys and mountains without extreme heat or cold. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through natural parks or protected areas?
A: Yes, several routes traverse significant natural spaces. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085610707/plazaola-greenway-and-leitzaran-valley-loop-from-tolosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plazaola Greenway and Leitzaran Valley Loop from Tolosa&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the scenic Leitzaran Protected Biotope. Additionally, the Aralar Natural Park offers tougher routes for those who enjoy challenges amidst beautiful natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes with good road conditions and low traffic?
A: For those seeking good road conditions and less traffic, the Alto de Iturburu climb is noted for its very good road conditions and low traffic, making it a pleasant yet challenging ride. Many of the routes through the green valleys and less populated mountain areas also offer a quieter cycling experience.
tips: Tolosa is a jewel of the Basque Country that stands out for its historical, cultural and gastronomic heritage. Its old town, the Plaza Nueva, the medieval bridge and the Parque de Aranzazu are some of its main attractions. Tolosa is also an excellent starting point to explore the surrounding nature, with beautiful mountains and trails.

Nice segment that is part of the beautiful 37-kilometre route along the old railway line that linked Pamplona with San Sebastián. The path stretches through the green valleys of northern Navarra and offers stunning views of the Pyrenees and the Basque coast. Ideal for a family outing.

This bridge is part of the Plazaola greenway and is known as the "witches' bridge". A well-known place for the inhabitants of the area. Quiet place where you can see the river, fishermen... it has some tables nearby to eat and spend the day.

The Leitzaran valley and the river of the same name (declared a protected biotope) with their clean waters and riverside forests, preserve a beautiful cultural heritage and natural splendor.

An incredible place to ride your bike.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Vía Verde del Plazaola – circular desde Tolosa por Andoain?
faqs.answer: This circular route is classified as difficult for touring cycling. While sections of the Vía Verde del Plazaola itself are generally gentle, the connection through Tolosa and Andoain introduces more challenging elements, including a 'hard' interruption to reach Andoain and some very steep slopes between Erice de Iza and Aizkorbe.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and surface can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows the old railway line, featuring gentle gradients. You'll encounter a varied surface: approximately 47% gravel, 15% unspecified surface, and 13% concrete, with other sections being compacted earth or asphalted. Be aware of an irregular surface between Andoain and the border with Navarre. The trail also passes through 53 tunnels, as well as over several bridges and viaducts.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience spectacular natural beauty, traversing lush landscapes with leafy slopes of beech and oak trees, rural environments, forests, meadows, and traditional farmhouses. Key highlights include the Tolosa Old Town, the Plazaola Greenway itself, the historic Witches' Bridge, and the protected Leitzaran River Valley. You'll also pass through numerous tunnels and over viaducts, and may spot restored old train stations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vía Verde del Plazaola – circular desde Tolosa por Andoain?
faqs.answer: Starting points in Tolosa or Andoain would be ideal for parking. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, both towns offer public parking options. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival, especially in the historic center of Tolosa.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Verde del Plazaola – circular desde Tolosa por Andoain dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, greenways in Spain, including the Vía Verde del Plazaola, are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in sections where you might encounter other cyclists, pedestrians, or wildlife, and to ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route can be enjoyed for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery with lush foliage. Summer can be warm, but the numerous tunnels provide cool respite. Winters are generally mild, but some sections might be damp or muddy. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this greenway?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit, and there is no entrance fee to access the Vía Verde del Plazaola. It is part of the Spanish Network of Natural Paths, which are freely accessible for recreational use.

faqs.question: Are there any sections of the route suitable for families or less experienced cyclists?
faqs.answer: While the full circular route is rated difficult, there is an 8-kilometer section near Andoain that is described as practically flat and suitable for families or those seeking an easier ride. If you're looking for a less challenging experience, consider starting and ending your ride within this specific section.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is an integral part of the Plazaola Greenway (Plazaolako bide berdea), and it also intersects with sections of the Leitzaran Bailara and the Camino de Santiago Vasco del Interior. It is also integrated into the EuroVelo 1 long-distance cycling route.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find amenities along the Vía Verde del Plazaola – circular desde Tolosa por Andoain?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near towns like Tolosa and Andoain, where you'll find various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and shops. Along the greenway itself, some restored old train stations now offer services and information to travelers, which may include refreshments or facilities.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, bring plenty of water, snacks, and appropriate cycling gear. Due to the 53 tunnels, some of which can be long and unlit, a good headlamp or bike light is essential. A basic repair kit and spare inner tube are also recommended. Layers of clothing are advisable for changing weather conditions.
